overview: A blue-black combo deck that leverages cheap mana acceleration and spell-copying effects to generate massive Storm counts. By combining Dark Ritual for early acceleration, Flow State for free spell casting, and Beseech the Mirror to duplicate key spells, the deck aims to resolve a high-impact finisher before the opponent can respond. The strategy prioritizes speed and spell density over board presence.
win_conditions: Resolving a high-Storm-count spell that deals lethal damage or draws the entire library
Using Beseech the Mirror to copy finishers and multiply their effects
Casting Flow State to bypass mana costs and rapidly accumulate Storm count

keep_criteria: Hands containing Dark Ritual and at least two combo enablers
Hands with Flow State and early land drops
Hands with Beseech the Mirror and cheap spells to copy
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking acceleration or combo pieces
Hands with only lands and high-cost cards
Hands without a clear path to turn one or two interaction

early_game: Play lands and cast Dark Ritual to enable turn one or two combo pieces
Set up Flow State and Beseech the Mirror while protecting them from disruption
Prioritize drawing into additional cheap spells to fuel the Storm count
mid_game: Cast Flow State to begin casting spells without paying mana costs
Use Beseech the Mirror to duplicate cheap spells and rapidly increase Storm count
Maintain a hand of spells to bait out opponent counterspells or discard
late_game: Resolve the primary finisher once Storm count is sufficient
If combo fails, use remaining resources to stabilize or draw into answers
Look for alternate win conditions like card draw or direct damage spells

key_cards.name: Dark Ritual
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn one to enable early combo pieces or protect Flow State from disruption

key_cards.name: Beseech the Mirror
key_cards.role: Spell duplication and Storm count builder
key_cards.notes: Use to copy cheap spells or finishers to maximize Storm count and damage output

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have multiple cheap spells in hand to rapidly build Storm count without spending mana

sequencing_tips: Cast Dark Ritual before Flow State to ensure you can cast or protect it
Use Beseech the Mirror after Flow State resolves to copy the free spells
Prioritize casting the cheapest spells first to maximize the number of spells in the Storm count
Hold up mana for counterspells if you suspect opponent interaction

matchups.advice: Play around counterspells by baiting them out with cheap spells before casting Flow State
Go wide with cheap spells and use Dark Ritual for early interaction to survive until turn two
Race for Storm count and prioritize speed over interaction
Use Flow State to bypass their resource advantage and finish before they stabilize
Finish quickly before they establish a large mana base and board presence
